summary: Iter8 full-stack verification. All 6 requested backend feature groups pass (10/10 iter8 tests). Full regression 97/97 (previous 87 + 10 new). Router extraction to routers/groups.py caused ZERO regressions — all previous /admin/groups, /me/groups, /public/availability, /waitlist, /admin/waitlist endpoints work identically. NEW endpoints verified: GET /api/admin/groups/suggest (returns groups w/ open slots, excludes paused/full), PATCH /api/admin/waitlist/{id} (status=contacted persists). Group status + expires_at accepted on create/patch. H-7 scheduler correctly (a) reports expiring_groups >=1, (b) sets expiry_reminder_sent=True, (c) logs group_expiry_reminder admin action, (d) idempotent on second run (0 additional). E2E Netflix group flow works: 2 users (host+regular) both see same credential email/password via /me/groups, is_me flag correct, members count=2. Frontend: [admin-tab-waitlist] tab visible with [waitlist-table] populated with 3 rows, each with [waitlist-contact-<id>] and [waitlist-delete-<id>]. GroupsTab modal has [gm-status] dropdown and [gm-expires] date input. SubscriptionsTab modal calls /admin/groups/suggest on service change; [submod-group] dropdown populates correctly OR shows red empty-state hint 'Semua group untuk service ini penuh...' when no available groups (verified via Netflix with no groups). FIXED during test: GET /api/admin/subscriptions was returning 500 due to legacy orphan sub with user_id='0' (invalid ObjectId) — added ObjectId.is_valid guard.

minor.issue: Was 500 due to un-guarded ObjectId(s['user_id']) when a legacy sub had user_id='0'. FIXED in this iteration by wrapping with ObjectId.is_valid(). Recommend also guarding all other admin_list endpoints similarly and adding a data-integrity cleanup.

Still requires full SubscriptionInput body (replace semantics). Frontend GroupsTab.assign uses PATCH with only group_id → would 422. Verify how frontend assign flow builds payload (may need to load existing sub then merge). Iter7 note stands.
Filters by g['active']=True AND status in ['active',null]. If a group was created without 'active' field pre-iter7, they will be excluded. Non-blocking but be aware for legacy data.
iter7 finding still applies (filled_slots now correctly filters group_id != null after iter7 fix — verified works).

ui_bugs.issue: Missing data-testid='submod-role' on the role <select> at line 119 — makes role-based E2E testing harder

action_items: MINOR: Add data-testid='submod-role' to role dropdown in SubscriptionsTab.jsx
MINOR: Consider hardening admin_list_subs style guards across other admin endpoints (users/payments) to prevent 500s on data corruption
MINOR: Investigate one orphan sub (user_id='0') in DB — consider migration/cleanup
critical_code_review_comments: routers/groups.py cleanly extracts 12 endpoints. Import-from-server pattern works because server.py exports symbols and mounts the router. Circular import avoided.
H-7 scheduler tick correctly filters {expires_at: {$ne: null, $lte: now+7d, $gte: now}, status:'active', expiry_reminder_sent:{$ne: True}} and marks flag idempotent — verified via test.
admin_list_subs 500 bug (fixed by test agent) shows lack of defensive ObjectId validation; recommend consistent pattern across all list endpoints.
PATCH /admin/subscriptions still uses full SubscriptionInput (replace not partial). Frontend must send full payload — confirm any callers still work.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — waitlist tab + rows + actions present, GroupsTab modal has status/expires fields, SubscriptionsTab suggest integration works with correct empty-state hint

seed_data_creation: Test users prefix 'iter8_' + groups 'Iter8_*'. Cleaned up via POST /api/admin/cleanup-test-users?prefix=iter8 at session teardown. All Iter8_* groups deleted in try/finally.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter8 fully verified. Backend suite now 97 tests. Run: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest backend/tests/ -o addopts='' -p no:xdist`. Iter8 module: `pytest backend/tests/test_iter8.py`. One backend fix applied (admin_list_subs ObjectId guard). Legacy orphan sub (user_id='0') still exists in DB — recommend cleanup. Twilio still MOCKED.
rca of the issue: GET /api/admin/subscriptions was 500-ing because ONE legacy subscription had user_id='0' (not a valid 24-char hex ObjectId). Root cause: admin_list_subs called ObjectId(s['user_id']) without validation, letting bson raise InvalidId which was not caught. Reproduction: `curl -b <admin_cookie> /api/admin/subscriptions` → 500. Fix applied: wrapped both user and service ObjectId calls with `ObjectId.is_valid(...)` guard, mirroring the pattern used elsewhere (e.g., my_groups). Post-fix: endpoint returns 111 subs, 1 has user=null. Test passes.
